International Women’s Day 2026 — 116 Pall Mall

# International Women’s Day 2026

## Accelerating Action at 116 Pall Mall

This year, International Women’s Day is centred around the theme “Accelerate Action”; a call to move beyond conversation and actively drive meaningful progress toward gender equality.

At 116 Pall Mall, action is reflected in our people.

Out of 71 team members across the business, 36 are women; just over 50% of our workforce. This places us above the wider UK hospitality average, where women represent approximately 52–55% of the overall workforce, but remain underrepresented in senior leadership roles, typically holding fewer than one in three executive positions across the sector.

Representation matters; but representation across all levels matters even more.

## Women Leading Across Departments

At 116 Pall Mall, women are not concentrated in one function, they are embedded across leadership, operations, culinary, sales, finance, and front-of-house roles.

From Ana, Deputy General Manager, to Charlotte, Head of Sales and Marketing, to department managers across the Directors Lounge, Brasserie, Events, and Business Centre, women are shaping strategy, driving revenue, managing teams, and delivering excellence daily.

In hospitality, women have traditionally formed the backbone of guest experience roles. However, industry research continues to show gaps in:

Senior leadership representation

Pay equity at executive level

Visibility in culinary leadership positions

That’s why having women represented in management, kitchen leadership, commercial strategy, and operational oversight is not just positive; it’s essential.

## Hospitality: A Sector Powered by Women

Hospitality is one of the UK’s largest employers, and women play a crucial role in its success. The sector offers dynamic career pathways — from events and sales to culinary arts and senior operations — yet progression can often be limited by lack of mentorship, flexible working structures, and visibility of female role models.

Accelerating action means:

Supporting career progression at every level

Championing leadership development

Creating inclusive workplace policies

Ensuring new talent sees clear pathways forward

At 116 Pall Mall, women lead event operations, oversee financial controls, manage premium guest environments, supervise bar operations, and deliver culinary craftsmanship. They are planners, strategists, supervisors, managers, chefs, and service professionals.

## More Than a Number

While 36 out of 71 means women make up the majority of our workforce, today is not simply about celebrating a statistic. It’s about recognising the contribution, professionalism, and leadership demonstrated every day across our venue.

It’s about ensuring that progress continues; that representation translates into opportunity, that leadership pipelines remain open, and that hospitality continues evolving into a sector where talent thrives regardless of gender.

International Women’s Day reminds us that progress doesn’t happen passively.

It happens when organisations choose to accelerate action.

Today, we celebrate the women of 116 Pall Mall and our continued commitment to building a workplace where equality, growth, and excellence go hand in hand.

Ariane Mercadal Bosch 10 March 2026

# BEST CONFERENCE VENUE

#### 116 Pall Mall

116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed Georgian building opened in 1828, serving as home to the Institute of Directors and one of London’s premier conference venues. Located on prestigious Pall Mall, it combines distinguished heritage with modern event capabilities delivered through warm Searcys hospitality.

Post-COVID transformation saw remarkable growth from 15 to 80 staff across seven departments, achieving 67% revenue increase from £4.5 million to £7.5 million. The venue accommodates 12 to 350 delegates, featuring the versatile Nash Room with stunning period décor and modern technical capabilities.

Our competitive advantage stems from central location, comprehensive service delivery, and holistic approach combining venue hire, catering, technology, and event management. Key innovations include hybrid event technology, ESG strategy with 20% waste reduction targets, new collaborative training suites, bespoke culinary experiences, and online booking systems generating over £95,500 revenue.

Under General Manager Rupert Outram’s dynamic leadership (GM of the Year 2025), the venue consistently maintains exceptional standards with 97.8% guest satisfaction ratings and perfect 5-star Google scores. Recent notable successes include the £96k AIEN Conference and recognition in Good Food Guide’s 100 Best Local Restaurants 2025, positioning 116 Pall Mall at the centre of London’s business conference landscape.

#### Central Hall Westminster

Central Hall Westminster (CHW) is one of London’s largest conference venues, offering 23 flexible event spaces that accommodate everything from intimate board meetings to conferences of up to 2,000 attendees. Its combination of heritage and state-of-the-art technology makes it uniquely attractive for delegates.

In 2024, CHW completed a £1 million AV and infrastructure upgrade, supporting high-profile events such as the Infected Blood Inquiry, which required full venue hire, live streaming, roof access, and extensive planning. Other standout events included Microsoft’s AI Conference and the EBRD Summit, which demonstrated the venue’s ability to deliver secure, complex, multi-room events.

Customer service remains a standout, with tailored service for each client, and is supported by expert AV, operations, and an award-winning catering team. Long-term partnerships and a 50% client retention rate underscore CHW’s commitment to excellence.

Strategic marketing efforts, including international exhibitions, showcase events, and new digital campaigns, attracted major new business, while membership in BEAM, AEV, and UVL positions CHW at the forefront of the industry.

#### Church House Westminster

Church House Westminster is a unique, Grade II listed, multi-purpose events venue in the heart of London. A stone’s throw from some of the capital’s most iconic landmarks, and with stunning views of Westminster Abbey, it hosts more than 800 events each year. This includes everything from conferences, product launches and award ceremonies to Christmas parties, receptions and filming. Accommodating up to 664 guests, Church House is home to 19 dynamic and flexible event spaces, expertly managed by inhouse event and production teams.

Church House ensures client needs are at the heart of its operations. Taking the time to get to know clients, and believing each one deserves a truly personalised experience, the award-winning venue builds relationships based on trust and transparency and provides a second-to-none service which sees clients return time and again.

Continually investing in expanding its offering and upgrading its facilities, and with a 10% discount for charities, it is committed to providing outstanding service at an affordable price. Once a base for Prime Minister Winston Churchill and the Houses of Parliament during World War Two, but now a hub of state-of-the-art facilities and the latest technology, Church House is the perfect blend of history and modernity.

#### Convene Sancroft, St. Paul’s

Convene Sancroft, St. Paul’s, the newest venue in the company’s portfolio, opened in Spring 2024. It is a 45,000-square-foot venue spanning two floors in a newly redeveloped Class A office building in London’s Paternoster Square and is ideal for major conferences with main-stage sessions and breakouts, trade shows, exhibitions, or large corporate receptions.

The venue comprises six unique meeting spaces – including a 900-person grand hall, the largest single above-ground meeting room in City of London – and can accommodate a full buyout of 1,200. Each meeting room can be custom-configured for various event types – from theatre-style for large conferences to classroom-style for interactive sessions, dining rooms for awards luncheons, and reception-style for social events. The venue thoughtfully incorporates plenty of gallery space for exhibitors, networking, and food breaks that include access to an unlimited snack and beverage bar. Common areas also offer cafe seating to enjoy meals prepared by a Convene Executive Chef.

In addition to providing beautiful spaces for a range of event types, the venue provides in-house catering with customization options, in-room AV and production supported by an on-site support team, and dedicated hospitality resources.

#### IET London: Savoy Place

Located in the heart of Westminster and home to 18 adaptable events spaces, IET London: Savoy Place is one of the capital’s leading events venues. It combines state-of-the-art AV facilities, a fascinating 800-year history, exceptional customer service and spectacular views of the River Thames to deliver purpose-driven events which are full of character, unparalleled in technical expertise, and undoubtedly one-of-a-kind.

With a firm belief in over-delivering, not over-promising, and with an approach centred around collaboration, Savoy Place works with clients to understand what they want to achieve. From Johnson Roof Terrace with its panoramic skyline views to the atmospheric charm of Maxwell Library, the venue’s eclectic offering has the adaptability, imagination and expertise to meet each brief with ease and enthusiasm. Catering for everything from conferences, exhibitions and product launches to award ceremonies, private parties and filming, Savoy Place builds events that are a true reflection of each client’s unique style.

This is enhanced by an expert in-house AV team and an award-winning catering partner, who take the stress out of event planning by bringing everything clients need under one roof.

#### National Gallery

The National Gallery is where creativity is found, connections are made, and inspiration is at every corner. Hosting your event at the Gallery means hosting an event in one of the greatest art galleries in the world.

The Pigott Theatre, located in the Sainsbury Wing, is our hidden gem. With recent renovation, it has stepped back into the spotlight. The space has been thoughtfully upgraded as part of the major Sainsbury Wing refurbishment, it is now one of the most versatile and comfortable conference venues in central London.

From the moment guests walk through the Sainsbury Wing entrance and into the light-filled foyer, the space for creativity and inspiration begins. The foyer, now double-height, is an open space that works perfectly for registration and networking. It looks out onto Trafalgar Square, and boasts a 12-metre 16K screen.

Heading down one level, you enter the Pigott Theatre Foyer. A light, bright and open area with a 300 standing capacity. The perfect adjoining space for refreshment and networking. Featuring built-in soft furnishings for comfort, a LED screen just outside the Theatre doors, expertly placed for live streaming, or for signage and agenda displays.

An elegant venue in Westminster overlooking the beautiful St James’s Park, One Birdcage Walk combines

## Introducing 116 Pall Mall

A special event calls for an extraordinary venue.

A stunning example of Georgian architecture and elegance, 116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed building presiding over one of London’s most prestigious addresses. Opened in 1828, it’s now home to the Institute of Directors.

116 Pall Mall also plays host to hundreds of corporate occasions, private events, festive celebrations and weddings every year, bringing a sense of occasion to even the most intimate gatherings. Guests are instantly captivated by the building’s luxurious interiors, complete with magnificent period features alongside its unique heritage and the impeccable service provided by our team.

We offer a wide range of versatile and flexible spaces throughout our five floors, welcoming up to 1,500 guests, and our enviably central location makes travel to your event a breeze.

François Bontemps

François Bontemps (1753–1811), later baron d'Abaumont, a brigadier general during the French Revolutionary Wars and the Napoleonic Wars. He was born 1 June 1753 at Saumur, and died 29 October 1811 at Saumur (Maine-et-Loire).

| Born | 1 June 1753 (1753-06), Saumur Maine-et-Loire |
| --- | --- |
| Died | 29 October 1811 (1811-10-30) (aged 58), Saumur Maine-et-Loire |
| Allegiance | - |
| Years of service | 1773–1801 |
| Rank | General of Brigade |
| Battles / wars | French Revolutionary Wars, Napoleonic Wars |
| Awards | Baron of the Empire, Legion of Honor. |

## Family and education

François Bontemps was born into a family of craftsmen, workers of copper and bronze, in Saumur. As a boy, his parents hoped he would become a priest and he was sent to study at the College of Saumur, and later Seminary of Angers. Lacking a vocation, however, Bontemps enlisted in an infantry regiment of the Royal Army. In 1784, having reached the rank of company clerk, and remained there for two years, he realized that he could not advance. He accepted a discharge the 4 May and returned to study the priesthood.

In 1789, François Bontemps embraced the principles of the French Revolution, and in 1792 enlisted as a chaplain in the volunteer battalion of the Eure. According to battalion lore, one of the officers of the battalion insulted the priesthood. Bontemps replied that he, as a priest, forgave him, but that as a citizen, he challenged him to a duel. He later killed the man in a duel and within a few months was elected as battalion lieutenant.

His intrepidity and leadership inspired his men to grant him the nickname of Bayard, after the famous medieval French knight. This same intrepidity, and his tendency to lead from the front, caused a serious wound from a musket ball on 3 May 1800 at the Stockach, by Engen. Despite this, he continued to fight and two days later participated in the Battle of Messkirch. Over the summer, though, his injury worsened and he was discharged from the army in 1801.

After his release from active service, Bontemps retired to Saumur, where he died on 29 October 1811. He is buried in the cemetery of Varrains.

## Dates of Service

- 21 Sept 1773: Regimental Corporal
- 1 Oct 1774 : Sergeant
- 13 May 1778: Sergeant of Chasseurs
- 4 May 1784: Decommissioned
- Early 1792 : Enlisted
- 1792 : elected Lieutenant of battalion, as part of the Armée du Nord
- April 1793: Lieutenant-colonel of 11th battalion of the Vosges
- 12 Mar 1794: Chef of Brigade, 175th Demi-Brigade, (Army of Sambre-et-Meuse)
- 5 May 1796: Chef of Brigade, 67th Demi-Brigade
- Sept 1797 : Transferred to the Army of Germany
- 1798 : Armée de Mayence
- 1799 : Army of the Danube
- March 1799: Général de brigade (promoted in the field at the Battle of Stockach, 26 March and officialized on 20 April).
- End of 1799 : attached to the Armée du Rhin
- 1801: released from service with serious injuries.

## Awards and honors

- 14 Jun 1804: Commander, Légion d'honneur
- August 1809: Napoleon gave him a silver snuff box and created Baron d'Empire title Baron d'Abaumont.
- August 1809 : Created Baron d'Empire with the title of Baron d'Abaumont.

## See also

- List of French generals of the Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars

## References

1. Georges Six, p.129

- Six, Georges (1934). Dictionnaire biographique des généraux et amiraux de la Révolution et de l'Empire, Vol. I(in French). Paris, France: Librairie historique et nobiliaire. ISBN 978-2-402-49916-3. cite book`{{}}`: ISBN / Date incompatibility (help)

# International Women’s Day 2026

## Accelerating Action at 116 Pall Mall

This year, International Women’s Day is centred around the theme “Accelerate Action”; a call to move beyond conversation and actively drive meaningful progress toward gender equality.

At 116 Pall Mall, action is reflected in our people.

Out of 71 team members across the business, 36 are women; just over 50% of our workforce. This places us above the wider UK hospitality average, where women represent approximately 52–55% of the overall workforce, but remain underrepresented in senior leadership roles, typically holding fewer than one in three executive positions across the sector.

Representation matters; but representation across all levels matters even more.

## Women Leading Across Departments

At 116 Pall Mall, women are not concentrated in one function, they are embedded across leadership, operations, culinary, sales, finance, and front-of-house roles.

# About us

## Introducing 116 Pall Mall

A special event calls for an extraordinary venue.

A stunning example of Georgian architecture and elegance, 116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed building presiding over one of London’s most prestigious addresses. Opened in 1828, it’s now home to the Institute of Directors.

116 Pall Mall also plays host to hundreds of corporate occasions, private events, festive celebrations and weddings every year, bringing a sense of occasion to even the most intimate gatherings. Guests are instantly captivated by the building’s luxurious interiors, complete with magnificent period features alongside its unique heritage and the impeccable service provided by our team.

We offer a wide range of versatile and flexible spaces throughout our five floors, welcoming up to 1,500 guests, and our enviably central location makes travel to your event a breeze.
